tdd_evidence.red_evidence: tests/test_openreport.py first run: 2 failed, 1 passed — AttributeError: 'Engine' object has no attribute 'open_report' at engine.open_report() call sites in both new tests.
tdd_evidence.green_evidence: tests/test_openreport.py: 3 passed; full py suite (src/python/tests): 55 passed.

implementation_notes.shape_d_rationale: Engine.open signature unchanged (returns just Engine); OpenReport stashed on PyEngine at open time, returned by clone via engine-attached accessor open_report(). Zero risk to legacy callers; sibling semantics to counters().
implementation_notes.openreport_type_choice: #[pyclass] frozen+get_all matches existing CounterSnapshot/SearchResult precedent in fathomdb-py and gives a clean .pyi story. PyMigrationStepReport + PyEmbedderIdentity are nested #[pyclass] types so per-step / identity fields are attribute-accessible from Python (e.g. report.default_embedder.dimension).
implementation_notes.idempotency_mechanism: OpenReport is captured once at Engine.open into PyEngine.open_report (Clone field); open_report() returns a clone each call. No re-probe, no live state.
implementation_notes.wrapper_layer: fathomdb/engine.py.open_report() transcodes the native pyclass into the fathomdb.types.OpenReport dataclass for consistency with counters() / search() pattern.

preexisting_blockers_unrelated: AC-037 netns-deny-egress (rootless userns+netns unavailable in sandbox)

next_step_for_orchestrator: codex review per orchestration.md § 3; on PASS / CONCERN-accept, FF main and push; OPENREPORT-ts slice unblocked (mirror engine.openReport() in napi binding, then flip parity matrix row to fully ✅).
